title: Just figured out an anagram for Gendo Ikari
display_title: Just figured out an anagram for Gendo Ikari
wikitext:
[]: Just figured out an anagram for Gendo Ikari             Quote Post by WunderBah » Sun Sep 14, 2014 10:46 pm "Ignore A Kid" How fitting!

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Sep 14, 2014 11:00 pm That's pretty good.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 14, 2014 11:17 pm The real question is whether or not the syllables of his name can be rearranged to create something meaningful in Japanese. I got nothin'.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Sep 14, 2014 11:18 pm Yeah, but it's funny in english. Hence the chit-chat designation of the thread.

[]:             Quote Post by TomasJC » Mon Sep 15, 2014 1:43 am Haha, nice. It seems his destiny was strangely pre-written, just like the Dead Sea Scrolls.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Mon Sep 15, 2014 2:22 am Either this is a huge coincidence. Or Anno is more of a genius than we could have ever anticipated.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Mon Sep 15, 2014 2:30 am Ray wrote: Either this is a huge coincidence. Or Anno is more of a genius than we could have ever anticipated.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Sep 15, 2014 2:31 am Yeah, but Gendo's hand just goes in Rei's boob.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Sep 15, 2014 2:25 pm It is also an anagram for "A Dike Groin". How do I know? I have the technology.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Sep 15, 2014 2:32 pm Gendou Ikari -> I do rue a king

[]:             Quote Post by ThanatosII » Mon Sep 15, 2014 2:39 pm Evangelion: Leaving One. This could mean either: 1) Gendo abandoning Shinji 2) Shinji leaving the single entity of Instrumentality 3) Both

[]: Re: Just figured out an anagram for Gendo Ikari             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Sep 15, 2014 2:40 pm WunderBah wrote: "Ignore A Kid"

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Sep 15, 2014 3:36 pm A Dirge Ikon A Rigid Keno A God Inkier Idea King Or

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Sep 15, 2014 5:47 pm First is funny, second is silly, third needs a spanking.

[]:             Quote Post by Veoris » Tue Sep 16, 2014 4:49 am TomasJC wrote: Haha, nice. It seems his destiny was strangely pre-written, just like the Dead Sea Scrolls.

[]:             Quote Post by Aether » Mon Nov 10, 2014 5:49 pm YOU'RE BLOWING MY MIND!!!! As for "Ikari", it means "anchor" in Japanese I believe, but in Hebrew, it means "central" or "primary". So they literally named the primary character "Primary".

[]:             Quote Post by strawhatpatriot » Fri Nov 14, 2014 9:51 pm That anagram could not be anymore fitting. Like seriously, Hideaki Anno must have planned this out

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Nov 14, 2014 10:29 pm No. No, he almost certainly didn't. These kinds of things pop up by chance, and then we give them meaning after the fact.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Nov 15, 2014 2:12 am And particularly so because it is in English, and there are multiple ways that names can be romanized.
